title: Odontarrhena penjwinensis
taxonomy: Odontarrhena penjwinensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, which includes coffee and gardenias. This plant is part of the genus Odontarrhena, which contains a small number of species. Odontarrhena penjwinensis is native to Myanmar, where it grows in the wild. The classification of Odontarrhena penjwinensis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships of this species.

intro: Odontarrhena penjwinensis is a flowering plant species within the family Boraginaceae, commonly found in mountainous regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Odontarrhena penjwinensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
